response: {"calibration_score":0.32,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":0,"well_calibrated_count":8,"biases_detected":["anchoring — plan.md treated as canonical artifact across multiple units causing repeated read-edit-verify cycles rather than a single well-scoped edit","re-edit thrash — units -6 and -8 each make 5+ sequential edits to the same file, indicating the initial edit strategy was not grounded in a complete read of current state","broad-first exploration — unit -4 issues 28 tool calls across shell, grep, and read before forming a clear hypothesis, with one failed read (use-sync-q.ts) that could have been avoided with a directory listing first","sunk-cost framing — once the plan.md document was created, subsequent units invested heavily in elaborating it rather than questioning whether the planning artifact was the right output format"],"recommendations":["Before any Edit call on a file touched in the same session, re-read the target range rather than relying on remembered state — the failed Edit in unit -8 and the re-read pattern in units -6 and -34 both stem from stale mental models","Cap exploration breadth at 5-7 tool calls before committing to a hypothesis; unit -4 ran 28 tools without a clear stopping condition, which is consistent with the [re-edit-thrash] pattern in mistake-patterns.md","Run tsc --noEmit earlier in the edit cycle rather than after all edits are complete; units -32 and -34 both defer type-checking to the end, which surfaced fixable errors late","When a document-editing unit requires more than 3 sequential Edits to the same file, stop and do a full Read before continuing — the alternating rg+Read+Edit pattern in units -6 and -8 is a diagnostic signal for insufficient upfront context"]}
